message:
fix(packs): block when any shell-segment of a compound command matches a destructive pattern

Closes the compound-command bypass class that the recent regression
test pinned: a user could chain a safe-subcommand prefix with a
destructive suffix via `;`, `&&`, `||`, or a literal newline, and the
pack evaluator would only see the first safe-subcommand match and
short-circuit.

New `split_command_segments(cmd)` splits on shell sequence separators
(`;`, `&&`, `||`, `\n`) while deliberately keeping pipelines (`|`)
whole so pack patterns that span a pipeline still match.

`PackRegistry::check_command` then:
- Runs the per-segment check against each segment. If any one segment
  is blocked, the whole compound command is blocked.
- Also runs the existing full-command check at the end so
  heredoc-aware / multi-segment pipeline patterns that legitimately
  span the whole input still get a chance to fire.

The previously-added compound-command regression test in the cross-
pack suite now passes for real; block is the evaluator's call, not a
regex accident.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
